calendar.create_event.description: Create a new event on the owner's Google Calendar. Requires an EA draft (`eaDraftId`) — the handler enforces that `actions.status = 'approved'` AND `ea_drafts.send_state = 'idle'` before sending. Supports attendees, conferencing, recurrence, and a private `ea_draft_id` extended property for idempotency recovery.
calendar.create_event.verifyNullJustification: Internal config skill — covered by RLS audit + service integration tests; deterministic post-check would duplicate existing coverage

eaDraftId.describe: EA draft ID for approval flow and idempotency

ownerUserId.describe: User ID whose calendar to create the event on

calendar.find_free_slot.description: Find available time slots in the owner's Google Calendar within a window. Uses the Google freebusy API to return gaps that fit the requested duration, respecting working hours.
calendar.find_free_slot.liveFetchRationale: Free/busy state changes constantly; must be live
calendar.find_free_slot.verifyNullJustification: Read-only skill with no observable side effect to verify

ownerUserId.describe: User ID whose calendar to query

calendar.get_event.description: Fetch a single calendar event by ID from the owner's Google Calendar. Returns full event detail including attendees, conferencing links, and recurrence rules.
calendar.get_event.liveFetchRationale: Event detail is mutable (attendees can RSVP at any time); reads must be live
calendar.get_event.verifyNullJustification: Read-only skill with no observable side effect to verify

ownerUserId.describe: User ID whose calendar to query

calendar.list_events.description: List calendar events within a time range for the owner's Google Calendar. Returns event titles, start/end times, attendees, and location. Supports optional filtering by calendar ID.
calendar.list_events.liveFetchRationale: Calendar events are time-sensitive; caching would miss same-day updates
calendar.list_events.verifyNullJustification: Read-only skill with no observable side effect to verify

maxResults.describe: Maximum number of events to return (max 250)

ownerUserId.describe: User ID whose calendar to query

eaDraftId.describe: EA draft ID for approval flow and idempotency

The canonical path is preferred and measured via planner.llm_skipped_rate; the live path is the fallback.

read_data_source.description: Query the run-scoped cascading context data sources. Two ops: `list` returns the manifest of all active sources visible to this run; `read` returns a slice of a specific source by id with offset/limit pagination. triage_intake.description: Capture and route incoming ideas, feature requests, or bugs into the task board. Two modes: 'capture' creates a single structured task in the 'inbox' (untriaged) column from raw text — fast, judgement-free intake used by the Orchestrator and Business Analyst when items arrive outside normal channels. 'triage' scans the existing inbox for items lacking a triage decision and returns a structured proposal list (duplicate check, rough relevance, suggested disposition) for human or Orchestrator review.
triage_intake.verifyNullJustification: Internal config skill — covered by RLS audit + service integration tests; deterministic post-check would duplicate existing coverage

input_type.describe: Classification of the input. Required in capture mode.

mode.describe: 'capture' for fast intake of a single idea/bug; 'triage' to assess and route the inbox queue

raw_input.describe: Raw text of the idea, bug, or feature request. Required in capture mode.

related_task_id.describe: Optional reference to an existing board task this is related to (capture mode), or the specific task to triage (triage mode with scope=single).

scope.describe: Triage mode only. 'all' processes the full untriaged inbox; 'single' (with related_task_id) processes one item. Defaults to all.

source.describe: Where this came from: human, support-agent, ba-agent, orchestrator, etc. Required in capture mode.
